title: pyrus buxifolia
intro: Pyrus buxifolia, also known as the box-leaved pear or Japanese quince, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antibacterial qualities, which can help to treat wounds and infections. The health benefits of Pyrus buxifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
